Performance Diagnosis: ADE - Trade

13 Apr 2026 PBI Performance Diagnosis Agent Mode: Hybrid

Analysis Approach

This report was produced by the PBI Performance Diagnosis Agent using an automated 9-source analysis pipeline. The agent examines the full stack — from Databricks infrastructure through dbt data pipelines to the Power BI semantic model and report layer — to identify performance bottlenecks and produce evidence-backed recommendations.

Data Sources & Tools

  • Active Semantic Model (Tabular Editor JSON) — Model structure, tables, relationships, measures, storage modes
  • Active DAX Measures Analysis — Complexity scoring, anti-pattern detection, context transitions
  • Active Best Practice Analyser (BPA) — 1690 findings across 12 rules
  • Active dbt Source Code (Serve + Curated) — Lineage, materialisation, clustering, column pruning
  • Active dbt Best Practices — 27 findings across 15 rules
  • Active Databricks Metadata & Volumetry — Row counts, table sizes, query statistics
  • Skipped Databricks Query History
  • Skipped Capacity Settings Simulation — Requires query history export (JSON/CSV) with per-query memory and row-set metrics; system.query.history does not expose memory consumption per statement
  • Skipped Workload & Infrastructure Analysis — Requires Fabric Admin API data (capacity CU consumption, surge protection config, workspace-to-capacity mapping) which cannot be queried programmatically; needs manual export from the Fabric Admin Portal
  • Active Column Memory Estimation — 599 removal candidates
  • Active PBIX Visual Layer Analysis — 67 visual rule findings
  • Active Synthesis & Cross-Reference — Root cause analysis, implementation roadmap

1. Executive Summary
45
Total Tables
9 DirectQuery, 29 Dual
801
Total Measures
49 critical, 22 high
1690
BPA Findings
132 high, 335 medium

The ADE - Trade semantic model (45 tables, 801 measures, 6.05 billion rows in fact_product_option_trade_daily_snapshot_v1) exhibits severe DAX performance anti-patterns across 378 of 801 measures, with 60 in critical tier (4+ anti-pattern flags). The dominant pattern is FILTER(ALL(Date)) combined with SUMX iterators in Last Year (204 measures), Last Week (116), and Average Weekly Cover (55) families — each generating 5-14 SQL subqueries per visual. The 6B-row Daily Trade fact table is in DirectQuery with 94 measure references and its serve view has no WHERE filter, meaning every PBI query scans the full 960-day history. The report has 12 pages with 274 visuals, all 12 pages exceeding the 15-visual threshold, and 19 wide matrices (up to 43 columns each). The #1 recommendation is to add pre-computed time-intelligence surrogate keys (LY, LW, WTD, MTD, HTD, YTD) to the Date table and refactor the 320+ LY/LW/Cover measures to use them, eliminating FILTER(ALL(Date)) and SUMX patterns entirely.

Key Findings & Recommended Actions (15 total — 0 critical, 0 high)

F1
Critical
320+ LY/LW/Cover Measures Use FILTER(ALL(Date)) + SUMX Iterators
Add pre-computed surrogate key columns to the Date table: dim_date_sk_ly, dim_date_sk_lw, dim_date_sk_lm, and boolean flags Is_WTD, Is_MTD, Is_HTD, Is_YTD. Replace SUMX + FILTER(ALL(Date)) patterns...
Semantic Model 80-95% reduction in SQL subqueries per measure; query time from 15s+ to 2-5s for typical Trade visuals
F2
Critical
6B-Row Fact Table Serve View Has No WHERE Filter
Add a date-based WHERE filter to serve_fact_product_option_trade_daily_snapshot_v1 restricting to the last 2 years (matching the PBI date filter window). Use a rolling window based on dim_date_sk.
dbt Models Semantic Model 50-70% reduction in rows scanned (from 6B to ~2.5B for 2-year window, further to ~250M if narrowed to current financial year)
F3
High
19 Wide Matrices With Up To 43 Columns Generate Massive DirectQuery Load
Reduce matrix column count to essential metrics only. Use drillthrough pages for detailed breakdowns. Split the 43-column Topline Performance matrix into 2-3 focused matrices on separate tabs.
PBI Visual PBI Report 50-65% reduction in DirectQuery queries per page load; proportional reduction in warehouse CU consumption

See section Detailed Recommendations for full implementation guides.

⚠ Join Cascade Impact

Any query touching Daily Trade can transitively involve 31 additional tables across 2 depth levels.

8 DirectQuery tables in the cascade generate separate SQL queries to Databricks, compounding latency.

Each relationship hop adds JOINs to the generated SQL. Deeper chains = more subqueries = slower response.

Join Cascade — Tables Transitively Involved per Query

When Power BI queries a table, all related tables in the snowflake model are transitively joined. Higher cascade = more JOINs = slower DirectQuery SQL.

Snowflake Optimisation Suggestions

  • Reduce branching factor for Option — high fan-out from hub tables means each query pulls in many dimension tables. Review whether all relationships are actively used by report visuals; unused relationships can be removed or deactivated.
  • Convert deep DirectQuery dimensions to Dual/Import — tables beyond depth 1 that are DirectQuery force additional roundtrips to Databricks. Small lookup tables (< 1M rows) at depth 2+ should use Import or Dual storage mode.
  • Remove bidirectional cross-filtering in branches (Daily Trade → Option Ranking, Option → Time Period, Daily Trade → Time Period) — bidirectional filters propagate across the entire snowflake chain, multiplying the number of SQL JOINs. Use CROSSFILTER() in specific DAX measures instead.

Hot Tables — Optimisation Candidates

TableStorageRefsRowsSizeDegreePriority
Datedual 216 10K - 2 medium
Optimisation Opportunities
  • Evaluate Import mode — Dual tables fall back to DirectQuery when joined with DirectQuery fact tables. If this table changes infrequently, switching to Import with scheduled refresh avoids repeated Databricks scans.
  • High reference count (216) — ensure the In-Memory cache is effective by enabling Large Storage Format and reviewing refresh schedules. A stale cache forces fallback to DirectQuery on every query.
Daily TradedirectQuery 94 6.1B - 21 critical
Optimisation Opportunities
  • Create aggregation tables — pre-aggregate at grain (e.g. daily/weekly) to avoid scanning billions of rows per query. Configure as Import with automatic fallback to DirectQuery for detail-level visuals.
  • Column pruning — remove hidden or unreferenced columns from the model. Each column in a DirectQuery table adds to the generated SQL SELECT clause, increasing scan width and query duration.
  • Simplify DAX references — this table is referenced by 94 measures. Consolidate similar measures or use calculation groups to reduce the number of independent queries hitting Databricks.
  • Hub table (degree 21) — this table is joined by many others, amplifying the cost of every scan. Consider denormalising frequently-used attributes into fact tables to reduce relationship hops and simplify generated SQL.
Daily Financial Transformdual 90 10K - 0 medium
Optimisation Opportunities
  • Evaluate Import mode — Dual tables fall back to DirectQuery when joined with DirectQuery fact tables. If this table changes infrequently, switching to Import with scheduled refresh avoids repeated Databricks scans.
Inventory Statusdual 40 22 - 1 low
Optimisation Opportunities
  • Evaluate Import mode — Dual tables fall back to DirectQuery when joined with DirectQuery fact tables. If this table changes infrequently, switching to Import with scheduled refresh avoids repeated Databricks scans.
UDAdual 28 2.5M - 1 low
Optimisation Opportunities
  • Evaluate Import mode — Dual tables fall back to DirectQuery when joined with DirectQuery fact tables. If this table changes infrequently, switching to Import with scheduled refresh avoids repeated Databricks scans.
Optiondual 26 2.5M - 9 low
Optimisation Opportunities
  • Evaluate Import mode — Dual tables fall back to DirectQuery when joined with DirectQuery fact tables. If this table changes infrequently, switching to Import with scheduled refresh avoids repeated Databricks scans.
  • High fan-out (degree 9) — multiple relationships increase the number of JOIN operations in generated SQL. Review whether all relationships are actively used by visuals; remove inactive ones to simplify query plans.
Daily Financial Half to Datedual 8 2.2M - 0 low
Optimisation Opportunities
  • Evaluate Import mode — Dual tables fall back to DirectQuery when joined with DirectQuery fact tables. If this table changes infrequently, switching to Import with scheduled refresh avoids repeated Databricks scans.
Daily Financial Year to Datedual 8 3.0M - 0 low
Optimisation Opportunities
  • Evaluate Import mode — Dual tables fall back to DirectQuery when joined with DirectQuery fact tables. If this table changes infrequently, switching to Import with scheduled refresh avoids repeated Databricks scans.
LW Slicerimport 7 - - 0 low
Optimisation Opportunities
  • No specific optimisation required — monitor query performance over time.
Product Lifecycle Statusdual 6 7 - 1 low
Optimisation Opportunities
  • Evaluate Import mode — Dual tables fall back to DirectQuery when joined with DirectQuery fact tables. If this table changes infrequently, switching to Import with scheduled refresh avoids repeated Databricks scans.
6. Best Practice Findings

Rule Violations

Impact: All(13)Latency(8)Cost(2)Memory(1)Quality(2)
RuleViolationsImpact TypePerformance Impact
UNUSED_COLUMNS_CANDIDATE 650 Cost Hidden/unused columns are still included in DirectQuery SQL. Increases read bytes and cost per query.
DAX_COLUMNS_NOT_FULLY_QUALIFIED 571 Quality Readability and maintenance issue. No performance impact but increases risk of errors when columns are renamed.
SNOWFLAKE_DQ_CHAINS 220 Latency Multi-hop DirectQuery joins prevent aggregation push-down and force chained subqueries. Each hop adds a nested SQL subquery.
IS_AVAILABLE_IN_MDX 115 Memory MDX exposure on non-attribute columns forces extra metadata and memory structures. On large DirectQuery models this increases model load time and query overhead.
FILTER_ALL_ANTIPATTERN 77 Latency Forces full table scan in DirectQuery mode. Each FILTER(ALL) adds an unfiltered subquery to the generated SQL.
DUAL_MODE_TABLES 25 Latency May trigger unnecessary DirectQuery when Import would suffice. Dual tables are processed twice during refresh.
DATE_TABLE_NOT_MARKED 11 Latency Unmarked date tables disable time intelligence optimisations and prevent efficient date filter caching in the storage engine.
MISSING_FORMAT_STRING 6 Quality No performance impact. Missing format strings affect display consistency and user experience.
WIDE_TABLES 4 Cost More columns increases bytes read per query. Primarily a cost issue; latency impact depends on whether I/O is the bottleneck.
AVOID_BIDIRECTIONAL_RELATIONSHIPS 3 Latency Generates additional SQL round-trips for bidirectional filter propagation. Can double the number of queries per visual.
TIME_INTEL_ON_DQ 3 Latency Time intelligence functions on DirectQuery tables generate multiple DQ queries per evaluation. On large fact tables this triggers full scans and severe latency.
CALCULATED_TABLES 3 Latency Calculated tables re-evaluate on every refresh, block query caching, and increase model maintenance overhead.
M_FOLDING_BLOCKERS 2 Latency M functions that block query folding force full data pulls from the source instead of pushing transformations server-side.

✓ 7 rules passed (no violations): AVOID_FLOATING_POINT_DATA_TYPES, AVOID_IFERROR, MANY_TO_MANY_RELATIONSHIPS, BARE_DIVISION, AUTO_DATE_TABLES, REDUNDANT_COLUMNS_IN_RELATED, EXCESSIVE_CALCULATED_COLUMNS

Detailed Findings (1690 total)

Impact: All(1690)Latency(344)Cost(654)Memory(115)Quality(577)
Severity: All(1690)High(132)Medium(335)Low(1223)
RuleSeverityImpactTableObjectDetail
AVOID_BIDIRECTIONAL_RELATIONSHIPSHigh Latency OptionOption.Dim Product Option Sk -> Option Ranking.dim_product_option_sk Relationship 'Option.Dim Product Option Sk -> Option Ranking.dim_product_option_sk' uses bidirectional cross-filtering. This creates ambiguous filter paths and can degrade query performance.
Why it's bad: Generates additional SQL round-trips for bidirectional filter propagation. Can double the number of queries per visual.
AVOID_BIDIRECTIONAL_RELATIONSHIPSHigh Latency Time PeriodTime Period.dim_date_sk -> Date.Dim Date Sk Relationship 'Time Period.dim_date_sk -> Date.Dim Date Sk' uses bidirectional cross-filtering. This creates ambiguous filter paths and can degrade query performance.
Why it's bad: Generates additional SQL round-trips for bidirectional filter propagation. Can double the number of queries per visual.

This creates ambiguous filter paths and can degrade query performance.
Why it's bad: Generates additional SQL round-trips for bidirectional filter propagation. Can double the number of queries per visual.

TIME_INTEL_ON_DQHigh Latency @MeasuresGross Tradeable Unit Cover L7D (Days) Measure 'Gross Tradeable Unit Cover L7D (Days)' uses DATESINPERIOD() on a DirectQuery-connected table. Time intelligence functions generate multiple DQ queries and can trigger full table scans.
Why it's bad: Time intelligence functions on DirectQuery tables generate multiple DQ queries per evaluation. On large fact tables this triggers full scans and severe latency.
TIME_INTEL_ON_DQHigh Latency @MeasuresReact HIt Rate LW % Measure 'React HIt Rate LW %' uses DATEADD() on a DirectQuery-connected table. Time intelligence functions generate multiple DQ queries and can trigger full table scans.
Why it's bad: Time intelligence functions on DirectQuery tables generate multiple DQ queries per evaluation. On large fact tables this triggers full scans and severe latency.
TIME_INTEL_ON_DQHigh Latency @MeasuresReact Hit Rate LM % Measure 'React Hit Rate LM %' uses PREVIOUSMONTH() on a DirectQuery-connected table. Time intelligence functions generate multiple DQ queries and can trigger full table scans.
Why it's bad: Time intelligence functions on DirectQuery tables generate multiple DQ queries per evaluation. On large fact tables this triggers full scans and severe latency.
CALCULATED_TABLESHigh Latency Column_selectorColumn_selector Table 'Column_selector' is a calculated table. Calculated tables re-evaluate on every refresh, block query caching, and increase model maintenance overhead.
Why it's bad: Calculated tables re-evaluate on every refresh, block query caching, and increase model maintenance overhead.
CALCULATED_TABLESHigh Latency LW SlicerLW Slicer Table 'LW Slicer' is a calculated table. Calculated tables re-evaluate on every refresh, block query caching, and increase model maintenance overhead.
Why it's bad: Calculated tables re-evaluate on every refresh, block query caching, and increase model maintenance overhead.
CALCULATED_TABLESHigh Latency OptionConditionSelectorOptionConditionSelector Table 'OptionConditionSelector' is a calculated table. Calculated tables re-evaluate on every refresh, block query caching, and increase model maintenance overhead.
Why it's bad: Calculated tables re-evaluate on every refresh, block query caching, and increase model maintenance overhead.
DUAL_MODE_TABLESMedium Latency Countrydim_country_v1 Table 'Country' is Dual mode and directly related to DirectQuery tables. Switching to Import would create a limited (cross-source) relationship with reduced functionality.
Why it's bad: May trigger unnecessary DirectQuery when Import would suffice. Dual tables are processed twice during refresh.
DUAL_MODE_TABLESMedium Latency Datedim_date_v2 Table 'Date' is Dual mode and directly related to DirectQuery tables. Switching to Import would create a limited (cross-source) relationship with reduced functionality.
Why it's bad: May trigger unnecessary DirectQuery when Import would suffice. Dual tables are processed twice during refresh.

DUAL_MODE_TABLESMedium Latency Price Statusdim_price_status_v1 Table 'Price Status' is Dual mode and directly related to DirectQuery tables. Switching to Import would create a limited (cross-source) relationship with reduced functionality.
Why it's bad: May trigger unnecessary DirectQuery when Import would suffice. Dual tables are processed twice during refresh.

FILTER_ALL_ANTIPATTERNMedium Latency @MeasuresTotal Retail Sales Buy Value - Prior Month Measure 'Total Retail Sales Buy Value - Prior Month' uses FILTER(ALL(…)) anti-pattern. Use REMOVEFILTERS or direct predicate pushdown.
Why it's bad: Forces full table scan in DirectQuery mode. Each FILTER(ALL) adds an unfiltered subquery to the generated SQL.
FILTER_ALL_ANTIPATTERNMedium Latency @MeasuresTotal Retail Sales Buy Value - YTD Measure 'Total Retail Sales Buy Value - YTD' uses FILTER(ALL(…)) anti-pattern. Use REMOVEFILTERS or direct predicate pushdown.
Why it's bad: Forces full table scan in DirectQuery mode. Each FILTER(ALL) adds an unfiltered subquery to the generated SQL.
FILTER_ALL_ANTIPATTERNMedium Latency @MeasuresTradeable Stock Buy Value LY WTD Measure 'Tradeable Stock Buy Value LY WTD' uses FILTER(ALL(…)) anti-pattern. Use REMOVEFILTERS or direct predicate pushdown.
Why it's bad: Forces full table scan in DirectQuery mode. Each FILTER(ALL) adds an unfiltered subquery to the generated SQL.
FILTER_ALL_ANTIPATTERNMedium Latency @MeasuresTradeable Stock Units LY WTD Measure 'Tradeable Stock Units LY WTD' uses FILTER(ALL(…)) anti-pattern. Use REMOVEFILTERS or direct predicate pushdown.
Why it's bad: Forces full table scan in DirectQuery mode. Each FILTER(ALL) adds an unfiltered subquery to the generated SQL.
WIDE_TABLESMedium Cost Daily Trade97 columns Table 'Daily Trade' has 97 columns (>50). May contain unused columns slowing refresh.
Why it's bad: More columns increases bytes read per query. Primarily a cost issue; latency impact depends on whether I/O is the bottleneck.
WIDE_TABLESMedium Cost Option63 columns Table 'Option' has 63 columns (>50). May contain unused columns slowing refresh.
Why it's bad: More columns increases bytes read per query. Primarily a cost issue; latency impact depends on whether I/O is the bottleneck.
WIDE_TABLESMedium Cost Product Option Country Restriction252 columns Table 'Product Option Country Restriction' has 252 columns (>50). May contain unused columns slowing refresh.
Why it's bad: More columns increases bytes read per query. Primarily a cost issue; latency impact depends on whether I/O is the bottleneck.
WIDE_TABLESMedium Cost UDA134 columns Table 'UDA' has 134 columns (>50). May contain unused columns slowing refresh.
Why it's bad: More columns increases bytes read per query. Primarily a cost issue; latency impact depends on whether I/O is the bottleneck.
MISSING_FORMAT_STRINGMedium Quality @MeasuresGross Tradeable Buy Value Cover (Days) Prior Month Measure 'Gross Tradeable Buy Value Cover (Days) Prior Month' has no formatString. Best practice requires format strings on visible measures.
Why it's bad: No performance impact. Missing format strings affect display consistency and user experience.
MISSING_FORMAT_STRINGMedium Quality @MeasuresLatest Data Available Measure 'Latest Data Available' has no formatString. Best practice requires format strings on visible measures.
Why it's bad: No performance impact. Missing format strings affect display consistency and user experience.
MISSING_FORMAT_STRINGMedium Quality @MeasuresOption Earliest Global Go Live Date Measure 'Option Earliest Global Go Live Date' has no formatString. Best practice requires format strings on visible measures.
Why it's bad: No performance impact. Missing format strings affect display consistency and user experience.
MISSING_FORMAT_STRINGMedium Quality @MeasuresSelected Week Measure 'Selected Week' has no formatString. Best practice requires format strings on visible measures.
Why it's bad: No performance impact. Missing format strings affect display consistency and user experience.
MISSING_FORMAT_STRINGMedium Quality Option RankingFirst Index Measure 'First Index' has no formatString. Best practice requires format strings on visible measures.
Why it's bad: No performance impact. Missing format strings affect display consistency and user experience.
MISSING_FORMAT_STRINGMedium Quality Option RankingStep Measure 'Step' has no formatString. Best practice requires format strings on visible measures.
Why it's bad: No performance impact. Missing format strings affect display consistency and user experience.
SNOWFLAKE_DQ_CHAINSMedium Latency UDAUDA → Option → Daily Trade DirectQuery snowflake chain detected: UDA → Option → Daily Trade. Multi-hop joins force chained DQ queries and prevent aggregation push-down.
Why it's bad: Multi-hop DirectQuery joins prevent aggregation push-down and force chained subqueries. Each hop adds a nested SQL subquery.

DATE_TABLE_NOT_MARKEDMedium Latency @Dataset Metadata@Dataset Metadata Table '@Dataset Metadata' appears to be a date/calendar table but is not marked as a Date Table. This disables time intelligence optimisations and efficient date filter caching.
Why it's bad: Unmarked date tables disable time intelligence optimisations and prevent efficient date filter caching in the storage engine.
DATE_TABLE_NOT_MARKEDMedium Latency Daily Financial Half to DateDaily Financial Half to Date Table 'Daily Financial Half to Date' appears to be a date/calendar table but is not marked as a Date Table. This disables time intelligence optimisations and efficient date filter caching.
Why it's bad: Unmarked date tables disable time intelligence optimisations and prevent efficient date filter caching in the storage engine.
DATE_TABLE_NOT_MARKEDMedium Latency Daily Financial Month to DateDaily Financial Month to Date Table 'Daily Financial Month to Date' appears to be a date/calendar table but is not marked as a Date Table. This disables time intelligence optimisations and efficient date filter caching.
Why it's bad: Unmarked date tables disable time intelligence optimisations and prevent efficient date filter caching in the storage engine.

Why it's bad: Unmarked date tables disable time intelligence optimisations and prevent efficient date filter caching in the storage engine.

DAX_COLUMNS_NOT_FULLY_QUALIFIEDLow Quality @MeasuresAdd to Bag Rate % Measure 'Add to Bag Rate %' references columns without table qualifier: [Action Add To Bag Count], [Product Views]. Fully qualify column references for clarity.
Why it's bad: Readability and maintenance issue. No performance impact but increases risk of errors when columns are renamed.

M_FOLDING_BLOCKERSLow Latency @Dataset MetadataTable.AddColumn, Table.TransformColumnTypes Table '@Dataset Metadata' uses M functions that block query folding: Table.AddColumn, Table.TransformColumnTypes. This prevents pushing transformations to the data source, forcing full data pulls.
Why it's bad: M functions that block query folding force full data pulls from the source instead of pushing transformations server-side.
M_FOLDING_BLOCKERSLow Latency ALB BracketTable.TransformColumnTypes Table 'ALB Bracket' uses M functions that block query folding: Table.TransformColumnTypes. This prevents pushing transformations to the data source, forcing full data pulls.
Why it's bad: M functions that block query folding force full data pulls from the source instead of pushing transformations server-side.
Show 1670 more findings...
7. DAX Anti-Pattern Tier Analysis

Measures scored by how many anti-patterns they combine. Multiple patterns compound cost because the engine cannot reuse cached results.

60
Critical (4+)
18
High Risk (3)
125
Medium (2)
175
Low Risk (1)

Severity Tier Summary

TierFlagsCountDescription
critical4+604+ anti-patterns combined; extremely slow
highRisk3183 anti-patterns; heavy use of FILTER + ALL + SWITCH
medium21252 anti-patterns; noticeable latency on high-cardinality visuals
lowRisk11751 anti-pattern; minor individually but expensive as dependencies
clean0423No anti-patterns detected
Anti-Pattern Catalog (9 flags)
FlagFunctionsWhy ExpensiveMeasures
ITERATORSUMX, AVERAGEX, RANKX, COUNTXRow-by-row evaluation; multiplies sub-queries in DirectQuery.57
ALL_FILTERALL, ALLEXCEPT, ALLSELECTED, REMOVEFILTERSClears filter context; forces full re-aggregation of the table.228
ROW_FILTERFILTERFull table scan; cannot use dictionary indexes in the storage engine.96
SWITCH_IFSWITCH(TRUE()), IFAll branches are evaluated by the storage engine; no short-circuiting.291
TIME_INTELDATEADD, DATESINPERIOD, SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR, DATESYTDCreates virtual date tables; adds joins and prevents caching.3
NESTED_CALCCALCULATE inside CALCULATEMultiple context transitions; each nesting adds a SQL subquery level.74
USERELATIONSHIPUSERELATIONSHIPActivates inactive relationship; forces alternate join path and prevents caching.0
CROSSJOINCROSSJOIN, GENERATE, GENERATEALLCartesian explosion; creates huge virtual tables that multiply query cost.0
DIVIDE_CALCDIVIDE(CALCULATE(...))Prevents single-pass aggregation; forces separate evaluation of numerator.14

Pattern Families

Measures grouped by naming convention and DAX structure. Each family has a consolidated "Why it's slow" and "Required actions" block.

Last Year Comparison Measures

critical 204 measures
ALL_FILTER ITERATOR NESTED_CALC ROW_FILTER SWITCH_IF

Examples: Average Line Buy Final Destination LY, Average Sales Units Per Day LY, Average Selling Price LY

Why it's slow

DATE(YEAR()-1) inside SUMX is row-by-row scalar evaluation. FILTER(ALL(Date)) scans entire date table. IF() evaluates both branches.

Required actions

  • Add LY_Date surrogate key column to Date table.
  • Replace SUMX with direct CALCULATE using the LY key.
  • Remove SWITCH(TRUE()) branching.

Last Week Comparison Measures

critical 116 measures
ALL_FILTER ITERATOR NESTED_CALC ROW_FILTER SWITCH_IF TIME_INTEL

Examples: Average Line Buy Final Destination LW, Average Sales Units Per Day LW, Average Selling Price LW

Why it's slow

Similar to LY/LM — bridge table lookup via CALCULATETABLE + FILTER(ALL(Date)).

Required actions

  • Add LW surrogate key directly to Date table.
  • Remove bridge-table lookups.

Average Weekly Cover Measures

critical 55 measures
ALL_FILTER DIVIDE_CALC ITERATOR NESTED_CALC ROW_FILTER SWITCH_IF

Examples: Avg Weekly Net Retail Sales Quantity (Physical Returns) (Saleable NRSQP Cover), Avg Weekly Net Retail Sales Quantity (Physical Returns) (Tradeable NRSQP Cover), Avg Weekly Net Retail Sales Value (Physical Returns) (Saleable NRSVP Cover)

Why it's slow

ADDCOLUMNS materialises virtual tables repeatedly. FILTER over ADDCOLUMNS doubles the cost. AVERAGEX over virtual tables multiplies sub-queries.

Required actions

  • Pre-aggregate weekly sales and stock into a weekly summary table.
  • Replace ADDCOLUMNS + FILTER with SUM / COUNT from the summary table.
  • Remove AVERAGEX by storing weekly averages directly.

Half-to-Date Window Measures

medium 16 measures
ALL_FILTER SWITCH_IF

Examples: Average Selling Price HTD LY, Average Selling Price HTD, Intake Units HTD

Why it's slow

Same pattern as WTD/MTD but scoped to half-year boundaries.

Required actions

  • Add Is_HTD flag to Date table.
  • Replace FILTER(ALL(Date)) with CALCULATE using the boolean flag.

Year-to-Date Window Measures

medium 15 measures
ALL_FILTER ROW_FILTER SWITCH_IF

Examples: Closing Tradeable Stock Buy Value - YTD, Gross Tradeable Buy Value Cover (Days) YTD, Net Retail Sales Value YTD

Why it's slow

Same pattern as WTD/MTD but scoped to year boundaries.

Required actions

  • Add Is_YTD flag to Date table.
  • Replace FILTER(ALL(Date)) with CALCULATE using the boolean flag.

Week-to-Date Window Measures

critical 11 measures
ALL_FILTER ITERATOR NESTED_CALC ROW_FILTER SWITCH_IF

Examples: Intake Units WTD, Net Retail Sales Value WTD, Retail Physical Return Quantity WTD

Why it's slow

SWITCH(TRUE()) evaluates all branches. SUMX iterates day-by-day. Nested CALCULATE inside SUMX. FILTER(ALL(Date)) forces full date table scans.

Required actions

  • Add pre-computed flags to Date table: Is_WTD, Is_YD1, Is_LW, etc.
  • Replace SUMX + FILTER with simple CALCULATE using boolean flags.
  • Remove SWITCH(TRUE()) by splitting measures by grain.

Month-to-Date Window Measures

medium 9 measures
ALL_FILTER ROW_FILTER SWITCH_IF

Examples: Closing Tradeable Stock Buy Value - MTD, Gross Tradeable Buy Value Cover (Days) MTD, React Hit Rate MTD %

Why it's slow

Same pattern as WTD but scoped to month boundaries. FILTER(ALL(Date)) forces full date scans.

Required actions

  • Add Is_MTD flag to Date table.
  • Replace FILTER(ALL(Date)) with CALCULATE using the boolean flag.

Last Month Comparison Measures

critical 4 measures
ALL_FILTER ITERATOR NESTED_CALC ROW_FILTER SWITCH_IF TIME_INTEL

Examples: React Hit Rate LM %, Retail Sales Value OB LM, Retail Sales Value T&R LM

Why it's slow

Same pattern as LY but for month offset. Bridge table lookups via CALCULATETABLE add extra joins.

Required actions

  • Add LM surrogate key directly to Date table.
  • Remove bridge-table lookups.
  • Replace FILTER(ALL(Date)) with TREATAS or direct equality.

Conversion Rate Measures

lowRisk 3 measures
SWITCH_IF

Examples: Conversion WOW %, Conversion YOY %, Conversion

Why it's slow

Typically divides two expensive aggregations, each generating separate DQ queries.

Required actions

  • Use DIVIDE with pre-computed VARs.
  • Ensure numerator and denominator base measures share filter context where possible.

Opening Balance Measures

lowRisk 2 measures
ROW_FILTER

Examples: Retail Sales Value OB, Retail Sales Value T&R Mix of OB%

Why it's slow

Uses bridge table lookups with FILTER(ALL(Date)) and IN operator.

Required actions

  • Add opening balance date keys directly to Date table.
  • Replace FILTER(ALL(Date)) with TREATAS or direct equality.

Last 7 Days Rolling Window Measures

critical 1 measures
ALL_FILTER NESTED_CALC ROW_FILTER SWITCH_IF TIME_INTEL

Examples: Gross Tradeable Unit Cover L7D (Days)

Why it's slow

DATESINPERIOD creates virtual tables. FILTER(ALL(Date)) scans full date table.

Required actions

  • Add rolling 7-day window flags to Date table.
  • Replace DATESINPERIOD with direct boolean filters.

Structural Pattern (ALL_FILTER+NESTED_CALC+ROW_FILTER+SWITCH_IF)

critical 8 measures
ALL_FILTER NESTED_CALC ROW_FILTER SWITCH_IF

Examples: Closing Tradeable Stock Buy Value - Prior Month, Commitment Buy Value Yesterday, Commitment Quantity Yesterday

Why it's slow

Multiple anti-patterns compound query cost.

Required actions

  • Analyse the specific DAX pattern and simplify.
  • Replace FILTER(ALL) with KEEPFILTERS or TREATAS.
  • Use VAR/RETURN to avoid repeated sub-expression evaluation.

Structural Pattern (ALL_FILTER+ITERATOR+NESTED_CALC+SWITCH_IF)

critical 7 measures
ALL_FILTER ITERATOR NESTED_CALC SWITCH_IF

Examples: ELC Intake Mix %, ELC Intake Rate %, React Hit Rate Dynamic Week%

Why it's slow

Multiple anti-patterns compound query cost.

Required actions

  • Analyse the specific DAX pattern and simplify.
  • Replace FILTER(ALL) with KEEPFILTERS or TREATAS.
  • Use VAR/RETURN to avoid repeated sub-expression evaluation.

Structural Pattern (ALL_FILTER+ROW_FILTER+SWITCH_IF)

highRisk 4 measures
ALL_FILTER ROW_FILTER SWITCH_IF

Examples: Intake Units LTD, Intake Units Yesterday, Return Units LTD

Why it's slow

Multiple anti-patterns compound query cost.

Required actions

  • Analyse the specific DAX pattern and simplify.
  • Replace FILTER(ALL) with KEEPFILTERS or TREATAS.
  • Use VAR/RETURN to avoid repeated sub-expression evaluation.

Priority Fix Order

#ActionMeasuresImpact
1Rewrite Last Year Comparison Measures204very high

6Rewrite Structural Pattern (ALL_FILTER+ITERATOR+NESTED_CALC+SWITCH_IF)7very high

9Rewrite Structural Pattern (ALL_FILTER+ROW_FILTER+SWITCH_IF)4high
10Rewrite Half-to-Date Window Measures16medium

12Rewrite Month-to-Date Window Measures9medium

14Replace FILTER(ALL(Date)) with KEEPFILTERS/TREATAS across all measures228high

8. dbt Best Practices dbt Models
14
High
8
Medium
5
Low
27
Total Findings

Rule Violations

Impact: All(6)Latency(5)Quality(1)
RuleDescriptionSeverityImpactCountRecommendation
E02 Missing liquid clustering on large curated tables
curated_dim_product_option_country_restriction_v1 → Curated model is incremental but has no liquid_clustered_by. PBI table has 2,565,480 rows.
curated_dim_product_option_fabric_composition_v1 → Curated model is incremental but has no liquid_clustered_by. PBI table has 4,740,271 rows.
curated_dim_product_option_multi_valued_user_defined_attributes_v1 → Curated model is incremental but has no liquid_clustered_by. PBI table has 12,689,340 rows.
 high Latency 6 Add liquid_clustered_by to the contract YAML with the primary filter columns.
E05 Functions on filter columns
serve_dim_product_option_in_warehouse_v1 → Functions on filter columns prevent predicate pushdown: cast
curated_dim_product_option_country_restriction_v1 → Functions on filter columns prevent predicate pushdown: lower
curated_dim_product_option_multi_valued_user_defined_attributes_v1 → Functions on filter columns prevent predicate pushdown: coalesce
 high Latency 6 Remove functions from filter columns; pre-compute values in the model instead.
E07 ROW_NUMBER subselect instead of QUALIFY
enriched_pricing_store_v1 → ROW_NUMBER() used in subquery/CTE without QUALIFY. QUALIFY avoids the extra subquery layer.
enriched_product_price_history_partner_v1 → ROW_NUMBER() used in subquery/CTE without QUALIFY. QUALIFY avoids the extra subquery layer.
enriched_product_price_history_retail_v2 → ROW_NUMBER() used in subquery/CTE without QUALIFY. QUALIFY avoids the extra subquery layer.
 medium Latency 6 Replace the ROW_NUMBER subquery pattern with QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER(...) = 1.
E13 Hardcoded magic numbers in SQL
serve_dim_product_option_in_warehouse_v1 → Hardcoded magic numbers in WHERE clause: 12, 23, 31, 59, 9999. Consider using dbt variables or named constants.
curated_dim_product_option_country_restriction_v1 → Hardcoded magic numbers in WHERE clause: 16. Consider using dbt variables or named constants.
curated_dim_product_option_v1 → Hardcoded magic numbers in WHERE clause: 12, 2, 31, 9999. Consider using dbt variables or named constants.
 low Quality 5 Use dbt variables or named constants for business-rule thresholds.
E04 No WHERE filter on serve view over large fact table
serve_dim_product_in_pricing_store_v1 → No WHERE clause on serve view over curated table with 211,597,318 rows
serve_fact_product_option_trade_daily_snapshot_v1 → No WHERE clause on serve view over curated table with 6,052,560,794 rows
 high Latency 2 Add date-based WHERE filter to restrict the scan window.
E15 Serve view that should be materialised_view
serve_dim_product_in_pricing_store_v1 → Serve model is a view over a DirectQuery fact table with 211,597,318 rows. Consider materialised_view to reduce repeated full-table scans.
serve_fact_product_option_trade_daily_snapshot_v1 → Serve model is a view over a DirectQuery fact table with 6,052,560,794 rows. Consider materialised_view to reduce repeated full-table scans.
 medium Latency 2 Change materialisation from 'view' to 'materialized_view' in the contract YAML.
Passing rules (9)

E01: SELECT * in non-prepare models, E03: Serve view wider than PBI model needs, E06: OR in JOIN conditions, E08: materialized: table in enriched/curated, E09: Missing incremental_predicates on large merge, E10: UNION instead of UNION ALL, E11: Nested subqueries >2 levels, E12: Plain Python UDFs, E14: Missing contract on public model

12. Detailed Recommendations

Implementation details, trade-offs, and alternative options for each finding.

F1: 320+ LY/LW/Cover Measures Use FILTER(ALL(Date)) + SUMX Iterators

Critical Model-wide Semantic Model

Why it's bad

FILTER(ALL(Date)) materialises the entire 9,964-row date table and scans it row by row. In DirectQuery, each SUMX iteration generates a separate SQL subquery. With 6 FILTER(ALL) calls per measure and a 43-column matrix showing 20+ measures, a single page load can trigger 200+ SQL subqueries, each scanning the 6B-row fact table. The bridge-table pattern (CALCULATETABLE + FILTER) adds extra JOINs to every subquery.

Affected Objects:
Tables: Date, Daily Financial Transform, Daily Financial Half to Date, Daily Financial Month to Date, Daily Financial Year to Date
Measures: Average Selling Price LY, Retail Sales Value LW, Avg Weekly Retail Sales Buy Value (Saleable BV Cover), Intake Units WTD, Net Retail Sales Value YTD, Retail Sales Value OB LM, Retail Sales Units Coversion % LY

Required action

  • Add dim_date_sk_ly, dim_date_sk_lw, dim_date_sk_lm columns to technology.serve.dim_date_v2 (join to self with offset).
  • Add Is_WTD, Is_MTD, Is_HTD, Is_YTD boolean flag columns to technology.serve.dim_date_v2.
  • Add dim_date_sk_ly, dim_date_sk_lw, dim_date_sk_lm to the financial_transform_daily_v1 bridge table.
  • Refactor 204 LY measures: replace SUMX + FILTER(ALL(Date)) with CALCULATE([Base], TREATAS(VALUES(Date[dim_date_sk_ly]), Date[Dim Date Sk])).
  • Refactor 116 LW measures: same pattern using dim_date_sk_lw.
  • Refactor 55 Cover measures: replace ADDCOLUMNS + AVERAGEX with pre-aggregated weekly summary or direct CALCULATE using weekly flags.
  • Refactor 51 WTD/MTD/HTD/YTD measures: replace FILTER(ALL(Date)) with CALCULATE using boolean flags.
  • Validate refactored measures in DAX Studio with Server Timings before publishing.

Estimated improvement: 80-95% reduction in SQL subqueries per measure; query time from 15s+ to 2-5s for typical Trade visuals

Impact: Critical Effort: High
Requires Deep Dive
  1. Profile the 10 highest-complexity LY measures in DAX Studio with Server Timings.
  2. Identify the common base measures shared across LY/LW/Cover families.
  3. Create a refactoring template for each pattern family.
  4. Refactor in batches: LY first (204), then LW (116), then Cover (55).
Trade-offs:
  • High effort — 430+ measures to refactor, requires systematic approach with measure templates
  • Date table changes require coordination between dbt and PBI teams
  • Must validate LY/LW offsets match current ASOS financial calendar logic
Options:
OptionDescriptionProsCons
Option A: Surrogate key columns on Date table Add LY/LW/LM offset columns to dim_date_v2 and use TREATAS for time intelligenceEliminates all iterators, single SQL subquery per measure, scalable to new time periods Requires dbt + PBI model changes, high measure refactoring effort
Option B: Pre-computed aggregation table Create a dbt model that pre-aggregates trade metrics by date with LY/LW/WTD columnsMinimal DAX changes needed, Import mode possible for aggregated data Adds data pipeline complexity, needs daily refresh orchestration, may not cover all 801 measures

F2: 6B-Row Fact Table Serve View Has No WHERE Filter

Critical Model-wide dbt Models Semantic Model

Why it's bad

Without a WHERE clause on dim_date_sk, Databricks cannot skip data files outside the queried date range. Even with liquid clustering on dim_date_sk, the serve view exposes the entire 960-day history. Every visual interaction — slicer change, cross-filter, page navigation — triggers a full-table scan of 6B rows.

Affected Objects:
Tables: Daily Trade (fact_product_option_trade_daily_snapshot_v1)
dbt Models: serve_fact_product_option_trade_daily_snapshot_v1

Required action

  • Add WHERE dim_date_sk >= (SELECT dim_date_sk FROM technology.serve.dim_date_v2 WHERE calendar_date = DATEADD(YEAR, -2, CURRENT_DATE())) to the serve view SQL.
  • Alternatively, use a dbt variable for the lookback window: WHERE dim_date_sk >= {{ var('trade_lookback_date_sk') }}.
  • Verify the PBI report's date filter window does not exceed the serve view window.
  • Test query performance before and after in Databricks SQL editor.

Estimated improvement: 50-70% reduction in rows scanned (from 6B to ~2.5B for 2-year window, further to ~250M if narrowed to current financial year)

Impact: Critical Effort: Low
Trade-offs:
  • Historical queries beyond the WHERE window will return incomplete data — coordinate with report authors
  • Requires dbt deploy cycle
Options:
OptionDescriptionProsCons
Option A: Rolling date window on serve view Add WHERE dim_date_sk >= 2-year rolling boundarySimple, immediate impact, no PBI changes Limits historical analysis to 2 years
Option B: Materialised view with partition Create a materialised view with date partitioningBetter query performance, Databricks handles optimisation Higher storage cost, materialised view maintenance overhead

F3: 19 Wide Matrices With Up To 43 Columns Generate Massive DirectQuery Load

High Report-specific PBI Visual PBI Report

Why it's bad

Each measure column in a matrix visual triggers a separate SQL query to the Databricks warehouse. A 43-column matrix with LY/LW variants doubles the query count. Combined with the FILTER(ALL(Date)) anti-pattern in F1, a single Topline Performance page load can generate 100+ SQL queries, each scanning billions of rows. This saturates the SQL warehouse connection pool and causes cascading slowdowns.

Affected Objects:
Pages: Topline Performance, Topline Performance Date, Weekly Trade Overview, Weekly Trade Overview Date, Daily Trade Overview, Seasonality Overview, Stock Coverage, Return Rates, New Options, Department Analysis (+2 more)
Visuals: matrix 0eac3eb0a1d581031ba4 on Topline Performance (43 cols), matrix ff330af6f5df06718cd0 on Topline Performance (17 cols), matrix b2e54f4d0d8dde750451 on Topline Performance Date (43 cols), matrix bf3a7ddbbdffc23c986b on Topline Performance Date (17 cols), matrix ce62ee30cfb59019be05 on Weekly Trade Overview (12 cols)

Required action

  • Reduce Topline Performance matrix (0eac3eb0a1d581031ba4) from 43 to max 15 columns — move detail metrics to a drillthrough page.
  • Apply the same reduction to Topline Performance Date matrix (b2e54f4d0d8dde750451).
  • For all 19 wide matrices, identify which columns are most frequently used and create a 'summary' matrix + 'detail' drillthrough pattern.
  • Consider using bookmarks to toggle between summary and detail views.

Estimated improvement: 50-65% reduction in DirectQuery queries per page load; proportional reduction in warehouse CU consumption

Impact: High Effort: Medium
Trade-offs:
  • Users accustomed to seeing all metrics at once will need to adjust workflow
  • Drillthrough pages add navigation clicks

F4: All 12 Trade Pages Exceed 15-Visual Threshold

High Report-specific PBI Report

Why it's bad

PBI loads all visuals on a page simultaneously. With 28-31 visuals per page, the browser sends 28-31+ parallel query requests to the capacity. The SQL warehouse connection pool (typically 10-20 concurrent queries) becomes saturated, causing queueing and cascading delays. Total page render time is bounded by the slowest visual multiplied by the queueing factor.

Affected Objects:
Pages: Topline Performance (28 visuals), Topline Performance Date (31 visuals), Weekly Trade Overview (17 visuals), Weekly Trade Overview Date (18 visuals), Daily Trade Overview (22 visuals), Seasonality Overview, Stock Coverage, Return Rates, New Options, Department Analysis (+2 more)

Required action

  • Audit each page to identify visuals that can be hidden by default or moved to drillthrough pages.
  • On Topline Performance and Topline Performance Date, group visuals into 2 bookmark views (e.g., 'Summary' and 'Detail').
  • On Daily Trade Overview (22 visuals), split into 'Overview' and 'Detail' sub-pages.
  • Enable 'Apply' buttons on slicers and filters to batch query execution (see F8).

Estimated improvement: 40-60% reduction in concurrent queries per page load; significant reduction in total page render time

Impact: High Effort: Medium
Trade-offs:
  • Report authors need to redesign page layouts
  • Users must navigate between views instead of seeing everything at once

F5: 3 Bidirectional Cross-Filter Relationships on DirectQuery Tables

High Model-wide Semantic Model

Why it's bad

Bidirectional cross-filtering doubles the number of potential filter paths in the model. In DirectQuery, this means the SQL generator must evaluate filters flowing in both directions, potentially doubling the number of JOINs in generated SQL. The Time Period <-> Date bidirectional relationship affects every measure that uses the Date table (216 of 801 measures).

Affected Objects:
Relationships: Option.Dim Product Option Sk -> Option Ranking.dim_product_option_sk (bidirectional), Time Period.dim_date_sk -> Date.Dim Date Sk (bidirectional), Product Option Fabric Composition.Dim Product Option Sk -> Option.Dim Product Option Sk (bidirectional)

Required action

  • Test Time Period <-> Date with oneDirection: verify all report visuals still work correctly.
  • For Option <-> Option Ranking: if bidirectional is needed for ranking cross-filter, use CROSSFILTER() in the specific measures that need it instead of model-level bidirectional.
  • For Product Option Fabric Composition <-> Option: same approach — default to oneDirection, use CROSSFILTER() where needed.
  • Test each change in PBI Desktop before publishing.

Estimated improvement: 10-20% improvement in query generation efficiency; reduced ambiguity in filter propagation

Impact: High Effort: Medium
Individual Analysis:
Option <-> Option Ranking
Likely needed for ranking cross-filter in Option View page
Fix: Use CROSSFILTER() in ranking measures only medium
Time Period <-> Date
Enables Time Period slicer to filter Date table and vice versa
Fix: Test one-directional — Time Period should filter Date, not reverse low
Product Option Fabric Composition <-> Option
Enables filtering products by fabric composition
Fix: Use CROSSFILTER() in fabric-specific measures medium
Trade-offs:
  • Removing bidirectional may break specific cross-filter visual interactions
  • Requires testing each affected page

F6: 35 Report-Level Filters and No Query Reduction Settings

High Report-specific PBI Report

Why it's bad

35 report-level filters add 35 additional WHERE conditions to every SQL query generated by every visual. This increases query complexity, reduces Databricks result cache hit rates (since filter combinations are highly specific), and prevents query plan reuse. Without 'Apply' buttons, each slicer interaction fires all visual queries immediately, multiplying the total query volume during user interactions.

Affected Objects:
Pages: (all 12 pages — report-level filters apply globally)

Required action

  • Audit the 35 report-level filters — identify which are truly global vs page-specific.
  • Move page-specific filters to their respective pages.
  • Remove filters that are redundant or no longer used.
  • In PBI Desktop → File → Options → Report settings → Query reduction: enable 'Apply' buttons on slicers and filters.
  • Disable cross-highlighting to prevent hover-triggered DirectQuery queries.

Estimated improvement: 20-30% reduction in query complexity; 50%+ reduction in interactive query volume from Apply buttons

Impact: High Effort: Low
Trade-offs:
  • Users must click 'Apply' after each filter change instead of seeing instant results
  • Disabling cross-highlighting removes the hover-highlight interaction

F7: 10 Dual-Mode Bridge Tables Safe to Switch to Import

Medium Model-wide Semantic Model

Why it's bad

Dual-mode tables incur double processing during refresh (both VertiPaq import and DirectQuery metadata sync). When measures reference these tables via FILTER(ALL(...)), the engine may generate unnecessary DirectQuery subqueries for what are effectively small lookup tables. The Daily Financial Transform table alone is referenced by 90 measures.

Affected Objects:
Tables: Daily Financial Half to Date (2.2M rows, no relationships), Daily Financial Month to Date (1.6M rows, no relationships), Daily Financial Transform (10K rows, no relationships), Daily Financial Year to Date (3M rows, no relationships), Earliest Global Available To Sell Date (10K rows, no DQ relationship), Earliest Global Go Live Date (10K rows, no DQ relationship), Time Period (1.3K rows, no DQ relationship), Weekly Financial Half to Date (16K rows, no relationships), Weekly Financial Month to Date (3.2K rows, no relationships), Weekly Financial Transform (1.2K rows, no relationships) (+1 more)

Required action

  • In Tabular Editor, change partition mode from 'dual' to 'import' for: Daily Financial Half to Date, Daily Financial Month to Date, Daily Financial Transform, Daily Financial Year to Date, Earliest Global Available To Sell Date, Earliest Global Go Live Date, Time Period, Weekly Financial Half to Date, Weekly Financial Month to Date, Weekly Financial Transform, Weekly Financial Year to Date.
  • Configure scheduled refresh to run after the dbt daily pipeline completes.
  • Test that all measures referencing these tables still return correct results.

Estimated improvement: Eliminates Databricks SQL queries for time-intelligence lookups in 90+ measures; sub-millisecond evaluation for bridge table operations

Impact: Medium Effort: Low
Trade-offs:
  • Requires scheduled refresh — data will have a refresh lag (typically minutes)
  • Import tables consume PBI capacity memory (minimal — total ~7M rows across all 11 tables)

F8: 77 Measures Use FILTER(ALL) Anti-Pattern — 571 Unqualified Column References

High Model-wide Semantic Model

Why it's bad

FILTER(ALL(Table)) in DirectQuery generates a full table scan subquery regardless of the user's slicer selections. In a composite model with a 6B-row fact table, each FILTER(ALL) call is a potential multi-second SQL query. Nested CALCULATE creates multiple context transition levels, each adding a SQL subquery layer. Combined, these patterns are the primary cause of the 15s+ query times observed on Trade reports.

Affected Objects:
Measures: Closing Tradeable Stock Buy Value, Retail Sales Value LTD > 0 Units, Retail Sales Value LTD > 40 Units, Retail Return Rate Quantity React, Retail Return Rate Quantity Test, Retail Return Rate Value React, Retail Return Rate Value Test

Required action

  • Address time-intelligence FILTER(ALL(Date)) patterns via F1 refactoring.
  • For non-date FILTER(ALL) patterns (e.g., FILTER(ALL(Option))), replace with CALCULATE(..., REMOVEFILTERS(Option), specific_filter).
  • Qualify all 571 unqualified column references with table names for maintainability.
  • Run DAX formatting tool across all measures for consistency.

Estimated improvement: Covered by F1 for time-intelligence patterns; additional 10-15% for non-date FILTER(ALL) refactoring

Impact: High Effort: High
Depends on: F1

F9: Product Pricing Store Dimension Has 211M Rows in DirectQuery

High Model-wide dbt Models Semantic Model

Why it's bad

A 211M-row dimension table in DirectQuery means every JOIN to this table scans hundreds of millions of rows. If any measure references columns from Product Pricing Store (even indirectly via RELATED), the generated SQL includes a massive JOIN that dominates query execution time.

Affected Objects:
Tables: Product Pricing Store (dim_product_in_pricing_store_v1, 211.6M rows)
dbt Models: serve_dim_product_in_pricing_store_v1

Required action

  • Analyse which measures and visuals reference Product Pricing Store columns.
  • If only current pricing is needed: add WHERE clause to serve view to keep only active records.
  • If full history is needed: create a narrow current-state materialised view for PBI, keep full history in curated layer.
  • Consider switching to Import mode if the filtered table is small enough (< 10M rows).

Estimated improvement: 90%+ row reduction if filtered to active records; significant JOIN performance improvement

Impact: High Effort: Medium
Trade-offs:
  • Filtering to current state may break historical analysis if any visual needs pricing history
  • Requires understanding business requirements for this table

F10: 650 Unused Column Candidates and 14 Unreferenced Columns on Daily Trade

Medium Model-wide Semantic Model

Why it's bad

PBI DirectQuery generates SELECT statements that include all non-hidden columns from each table involved in the query. Unreferenced columns on the 6B-row fact table increase read_bytes per query without providing any analytical value. Each unnecessary column adds ~18 GB of scanning per full-table query (6B rows × ~3 bytes average per column).

Affected Objects:
Tables: Daily Trade (14 removal candidates), Product Pricing Store (7 candidates), Option (46 candidates), UDA (118 candidates), Product Option In Pricing Store (10 candidates)
Columns: Daily Trade.Physical Retail Return Buy Value Exc Tax, Daily Trade.Physical Retail Return Buy Value Tax Value, Daily Trade.Physical Retail Return Margin, Daily Trade.Physical Retail Return Realised Markdown Exc Tax, Daily Trade.Physical Retail Return Unit Cost Value

Required action

  • Review the 14 Daily Trade removal candidates with the report author to confirm they are not needed.
  • Set isHidden = true for confirmed unused columns in Tabular Editor.
  • Review the 118 UDA removal candidates — this dimension has 134 columns, most from wide PIVOT operations.
  • Consider creating a narrow serve view for the UDA table with only referenced columns.

Estimated improvement: 10-15% reduction in read_bytes for queries involving Daily Trade; larger savings for UDA table queries

Impact: Medium Effort: Low
Trade-offs:
  • Must verify columns are truly unused before hiding — some may be used in report-level filters or visual tooltips not detected by the script

F11: 5 Amplification Chains: Cover Measures Calling LW Measures Inside Iterators

Critical Model-wide Semantic Model

Why it's bad

When a critical-tier measure (4+ anti-patterns) calls another expensive measure inside an iterator (SUMX/AVERAGEX), the cost is multiplicative, not additive. If the outer measure iterates over 52 weeks and the inner measure generates 5 subqueries per evaluation, the total is 260 subqueries for a single cell in a matrix visual.

Affected Objects:
Measures: Avg Weekly Retail Sales Buy Value LW (Saleable BV Cover), Avg Weekly Retail Sales Buy Value LW (Tradeable BV Cover), Avg Weekly Retail Sales Buy Value LY (Saleable BV Cover), Avg Weekly Retail Sales Buy Value LY (Tradeable BV Cover), Avg Weekly Retail Sales Quantity LW (Saleable RSQ Cover)

Required action

  • Refactor base LW/LY measures first (F1) to reduce per-call cost.
  • Then refactor Cover measures to use pre-aggregated weekly summary table instead of ADDCOLUMNS + AVERAGEX.
  • Create weekly_trade_summary dbt model aggregating sales/stock by week.
  • Replace AVERAGEX(ADDCOLUMNS(...)) with AVERAGE from the summary table.

Estimated improvement: 95%+ subquery reduction for Cover measures; from 260+ subqueries to 2-3 per measure evaluation

Impact: Critical Effort: High
Depends on: F1
Trade-offs:
  • Requires weekly summary table creation in dbt
  • Must ensure weekly aggregation aligns with ASOS financial calendar (Mon-Sun vs Sun-Sat)

F12: 6 Large Curated Tables Missing Liquid Clustering

Medium Model-wide dbt Models Semantic Model

Why it's bad

Without liquid clustering on the JOIN key, Databricks must scan all data files to find matching rows during JOINs. For tables with 2.5-12.7M rows, this means reading more files than necessary, increasing I/O latency per DirectQuery query.

Affected Objects:
dbt Models: curated_dim_product_option_country_restriction_v1 (2.6M rows), curated_dim_product_option_fabric_composition_v1 (4.7M rows), curated_dim_product_option_multi_valued_user_defined_attributes_v1 (12.7M rows), curated_dim_product_option_single_valued_user_defined_attributes_v1 (2.5M rows), curated_dim_product_option_v1 (2.5M rows)

Required action

  • Add liquid_clustered_by: ['dim_product_option_sk'] to curated_dim_product_option_country_restriction_v1 contract.
  • Same for curated_dim_product_option_fabric_composition_v1, curated_dim_product_option_multi_valued_user_defined_attributes_v1, curated_dim_product_option_single_valued_user_defined_attributes_v1, curated_dim_product_option_v1.
  • Run dbt build for affected models to apply clustering.
  • Verify clustering was applied with DESCRIBE DETAIL.

Estimated improvement: 30-50% reduction in file reads for JOINs against these tables; most impactful for Product Option tables used in many Trade measures

Impact: Medium Effort: Low
Trade-offs:
  • Requires dbt run to apply — will be picked up on next full refresh
  • Clustering reorganises data files which takes compute time on first run

F13: 3 Calculated Tables Add Unnecessary Model Complexity

Low Model-wide Semantic Model

Why it's bad

Calculated tables are fully re-evaluated on every model refresh. If they reference DirectQuery tables, the refresh must query Databricks to build them. They also consume VertiPaq memory during refresh processing.

Affected Objects:
Tables: ALB Bracket (calculated), LW Slicer (calculated), OptionConditionSelector (calculated)

Required action

  • Check if ALB Bracket, LW Slicer, and OptionConditionSelector reference any DirectQuery tables.
  • If they are simple static lookups, convert to Import tables with manual entry or CSV source.
  • If they contain dynamic calculations, evaluate whether the calculation can be moved to a measure.

Estimated improvement: Minimal direct performance gain — primarily a model hygiene improvement

Impact: Low Effort: Low
Trade-offs:
  • Low priority — address after higher-impact findings

F14: 220 Snowflake DQ Chain Violations Across Model Relationships

Medium Model-wide Semantic Model

Why it's bad

Each relationship hop in a snowflake schema adds a JOIN to the DirectQuery SQL. Deep chains (3-4 levels) with large intermediate tables compound the scan cost. The Option table (2.5M rows, degree 21) participates in virtually every query that involves product attributes, multiplying the JOIN cost.

Affected Objects:
Tables: Daily Trade (fact), Option (hub, degree 21), Date (hub, degree 2), Product Pricing Store, Product Option In Store, Product Option In Warehouse

Required action

  • Focus on F1 and F2 first — addressing DAX patterns and serve view filtering will have far greater impact.
  • For long-term architecture: evaluate which product dimension chains are most frequently queried.
  • Consider creating a flattened product dimension that combines Option + key attributes into a single Import table.

Estimated improvement: Addressed indirectly by other findings; standalone snowflake flattening would provide 10-20% query improvement for product-dimension-heavy visuals

Impact: Medium Effort: High
Depends on: F1 F2
Trade-offs:
  • Denormalising the product snowflake is a major model redesign with high risk of breaking existing measures
  • Not recommended as a standalone initiative

F15: Queries Cross 5 Databricks Catalogs Per Visual

Medium Model-wide Semantic Model

Why it's bad

Cross-catalog JOINs in Databricks require the query optimizer to coordinate data access across multiple metastore objects. While functionally transparent, the query planning phase may take longer and predicate pushdown optimisations may be limited across catalog boundaries.

Affected Objects:
Tables: sales.serve.fact_product_option_trade_daily_snapshot_v1, product.serve.dim_product_option_v1, technology.serve.dim_date_v2, supplychain.serve.dim_warehouse_v1, sourcingandbuying.serve.dim_supplier_v1

Required action

  • No immediate action required — this is an architectural constraint.
  • For long-term consideration: evaluate whether Trade-specific tables could be co-located in a single catalog via views or materialised views.

Estimated improvement: Minimal standalone impact; addressed by F1 and F2

Impact: Medium Effort: Low
Trade-offs:
  • Changing catalog structure is an infrastructure-level decision beyond this model's scope

Location: Trade report-level filters · Finding: F6		 High PBI Report Synthesis High Consolidate 35 report-level filters — move page-specific filters to page level, remove unused

Location: serve_dim_product_in_pricing_store_v1 · Finding: F9		 Medium dbt Models Semantic Model Synthesis High Add WHERE filter to serve_dim_product_in_pricing_store_v1 for active records only

ACT-011 Split Topline Performance Date page (31 visuals) into Summary + Detail bookmark views

Location: Time Period <-> Date relationship · Finding: F5		 Low Semantic Model Synthesis Medium Review and set Time Period <-> Date relationship to oneDirection
ACT-015 Add dim_date_sk_ly, dim_date_sk_lw, dim_date_sk_lm surrogate key columns to technology.serve.dim_date_v2
Location: technology.serve.dim_date_v2 · Finding: F1		 Low dbt Models Semantic Model Synthesis Critical Add dim_date_sk_ly, dim_date_sk_lw, dim_date_sk_lm surrogate key columns to technology.serve.dim_date_v2
ACT-016 Add Is_WTD, Is_MTD, Is_HTD, Is_YTD boolean flags to technology.serve.dim_date_v2
Location: technology.serve.dim_date_v2 · Finding: F1		 Low dbt Models Semantic Model Synthesis Critical Add Is_WTD, Is_MTD, Is_HTD, Is_YTD boolean flags to technology.serve.dim_date_v2
ACT-017 Refactor 204 Last Year measures to use dim_date_sk_ly with TREATAS
Location: @Measures (204 LY measures) · Finding: F1		 Low Semantic Model Synthesis Critical Refactor 204 Last Year measures to use dim_date_sk_ly with TREATAS
ACT-018 Refactor 116 Last Week measures to use dim_date_sk_lw
Location: @Measures (116 LW measures) · Finding: F1		 Low Semantic Model Synthesis Critical Refactor 116 Last Week measures to use dim_date_sk_lw
ACT-019 Refactor 55 Cover measures — pre-aggregate weekly summary or use CALCULATE with weekly flags
Location: @Measures (55 Cover measures) · Finding: F1, F11		 Low Semantic Model Synthesis Critical Refactor 55 Cover measures — pre-aggregate weekly summary or use CALCULATE with weekly flags
ACT-020 Refactor 51 WTD/MTD/HTD/YTD measures to use boolean flags instead of FILTER(ALL(Date))
Location: @Measures (51 to-date measures) · Finding: F1		 Low Semantic Model Synthesis High Refactor 51 WTD/MTD/HTD/YTD measures to use boolean flags instead of FILTER(ALL(Date))
ACT-021 Create weekly_trade_summary dbt model for Cover measure pre-aggregation
Location: sales domain · Finding: F11		 Low dbt Models Semantic Model Synthesis High Create weekly_trade_summary dbt model for Cover measure pre-aggregation
ACT-022 Fix 3 AVOID_BIDIRECTIONAL_RELATIONSHIPS violations
Location: 3 objects · Finding: AVOID_BIDIRECTIONAL_RELATIONSHIPS		 Medium Semantic Model BPA Generates additional SQL round-trips for bidirectional filter propagation. Can double the number of queries per visual. —
ACT-023 Fix 25 DUAL_MODE_TABLES violations
Location: 25 objects · Finding: DUAL_MODE_TABLES		 Medium Semantic Model BPA May trigger unnecessary DirectQuery when Import would suffice. Dual tables are processed twice during refresh. —
ACT-024 Fix 77 FILTER_ALL_ANTIPATTERN violations
Location: 77 objects · Finding: FILTER_ALL_ANTIPATTERN		 Medium Semantic Model BPA Forces full table scan in DirectQuery mode. Each FILTER(ALL) adds an unfiltered subquery to the generated SQL. —
ACT-025 Fix 4 WIDE_TABLES violations
Location: 4 objects · Finding: WIDE_TABLES		 Medium Semantic Model BPA More columns increases bytes read per query. Primarily a cost issue; latency impact depends on whether I/O is the bottleneck. —
ACT-026 Fix 6 MISSING_FORMAT_STRING violations
Location: 6 objects · Finding: MISSING_FORMAT_STRING		 Medium Semantic Model BPA No performance impact. Missing format strings affect display consistency and user experience. —
ACT-027 Fix 571 DAX_COLUMNS_NOT_FULLY_QUALIFIED violations
Location: 571 objects · Finding: DAX_COLUMNS_NOT_FULLY_QUALIFIED		 Medium Semantic Model BPA Readability and maintenance issue. No performance impact but increases risk of errors when columns are renamed. —
ACT-028 Fix 650 UNUSED_COLUMNS_CANDIDATE violations
Location: 650 objects · Finding: UNUSED_COLUMNS_CANDIDATE		 Medium Semantic Model BPA Hidden/unused columns are still included in DirectQuery SQL. Increases read bytes and cost per query. —
ACT-029 Fix 115 IS_AVAILABLE_IN_MDX violations
Location: 115 objects · Finding: IS_AVAILABLE_IN_MDX		 Medium Semantic Model BPA MDX exposure on non-attribute columns forces extra metadata and memory structures. On large DirectQuery models this increases model load time and query overhead. —
ACT-030 Fix 3 TIME_INTEL_ON_DQ violations
Location: 3 objects · Finding: TIME_INTEL_ON_DQ		 Medium Semantic Model BPA Time intelligence functions on DirectQuery tables generate multiple DQ queries per evaluation. On large fact tables this triggers full scans and severe latency. —
ACT-031 Fix 3 CALCULATED_TABLES violations
Location: 3 objects · Finding: CALCULATED_TABLES		 Medium Semantic Model BPA Calculated tables re-evaluate on every refresh, block query caching, and increase model maintenance overhead. —
ACT-032 Fix 220 SNOWFLAKE_DQ_CHAINS violations
Location: 220 objects · Finding: SNOWFLAKE_DQ_CHAINS		 Medium Semantic Model BPA Multi-hop DirectQuery joins prevent aggregation push-down and force chained subqueries. Each hop adds a nested SQL subquery. —
ACT-033 Fix 11 DATE_TABLE_NOT_MARKED violations
Location: 11 objects · Finding: DATE_TABLE_NOT_MARKED		 Medium Semantic Model BPA Unmarked date tables disable time intelligence optimisations and prevent efficient date filter caching in the storage engine. —
ACT-034 Fix 2 M_FOLDING_BLOCKERS violations
Location: 2 objects · Finding: M_FOLDING_BLOCKERS		 Medium Semantic Model BPA M functions that block query folding force full data pulls from the source instead of pushing transformations server-side. —
ACT-035 Fix 6 E02 (Missing liquid clustering on large curated tables) violations
Location: 6 models · Finding: E02		 High dbt Models Semantic Model Engineering BPA — Add liquid_clustered_by to the contract YAML with the primary filter columns.
ACT-036 Fix 2 E04 (No WHERE filter on serve view over large fact table) violations
Location: 2 models · Finding: E04		 High dbt Models Semantic Model Engineering BPA — Add date-based WHERE filter to restrict the scan window.
ACT-037 Fix 6 E05 (Functions on filter columns) violations
Location: 6 models · Finding: E05		 High dbt Models Semantic Model Engineering BPA — Remove functions from filter columns; pre-compute values in the model instead.
ACT-038 Fix 6 E07 (ROW_NUMBER subselect instead of QUALIFY) violations
Location: 6 models · Finding: E07		 Medium dbt Models Semantic Model Engineering BPA — Replace the ROW_NUMBER subquery pattern with QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER(...) = 1.
ACT-039 Fix 5 E13 (Hardcoded magic numbers in SQL) violations
Location: 5 models · Finding: E13		 Low dbt Models Semantic Model Engineering BPA — Use dbt variables or named constants for business-rule thresholds.
ACT-040 Fix 2 E15 (Serve view that should be materialised_view) violations
Location: 2 models · Finding: E15		 Medium dbt Models Semantic Model Engineering BPA — Change materialisation from 'view' to 'materialized_view' in the contract YAML.
ACT-041 Fix 1 V03 (Excessive report-level filters) findings in Ranking View of Trade
Location: Ranking View of Trade · Finding: V03		 Medium PBI Report Visual Analysis 18 report-level filters (threshold: 10) Consolidate report-level filters. Move page-specific filters to page level
ACT-042 Fix 1 V08 (Large embedded images) findings in Ranking View of Trade
Location: Ranking View of Trade · Finding: V08		 Low PBI Report Visual Analysis 1 image visual(s) Use URL-referenced images hosted on a CDN instead of embedded images. Compress backgrounds to <200 KB
ACT-043 Fix 5 V01 (Too many visuals per page) findings in Sales
Location: Sales · Finding: V01		 Medium PBI Report Visual Analysis 23 visual containers (threshold: 15) Split into sub-pages or use bookmarks to show/hide groups of visuals
ACT-044 Fix 2 V02 (No date slicer on data-heavy pages) findings in Sales
Location: Sales · Finding: V02		 Medium PBI Report Visual Analysis Page contains pivotTable visual(s) but no date slicer Add a date slicer or relative date filter to enable partition pruning on DirectQuery tables
ACT-045 Fix 1 V03 (Excessive report-level filters) findings in Sales
Location: Sales · Finding: V03		 Medium PBI Report Visual Analysis 30 report-level filters (threshold: 10) Consolidate report-level filters. Move page-specific filters to page level
ACT-046 Fix 1 V05 (Auto-refresh or query reduction settings) findings in Sales
Location: Sales · Finding: V05		 Medium PBI Report Visual Analysis Query reduction settings not fully optimised: Slicer 'Apply' button disabled — each slicer selection immediately fires queries instead of batching; Filter 'Apply' button disabled — each filter change immediately fires queries In PBI Desktop → File → Options → Report settings → Query reduction: consider disabling cross-highlighting and enabling 'Apply' buttons on slicers and filters to reduce DirectQuery query volume during user interaction.
ACT-047 Fix 1 V06 (Wide tables/matrices) findings in Sales
Location: Sales · Finding: V06		 Medium PBI Report Visual Analysis table visual 'a4a85c0b88a26d08bbf4' has 11 projected columns (threshold: 10) Reduce column count to essential fields. Use drillthrough pages for detail views
ACT-048 Fix 10 V08 (Large embedded images) findings in Sales
Location: Sales · Finding: V08		 Low PBI Report Visual Analysis 1 image visual(s) Use URL-referenced images hosted on a CDN instead of embedded images. Compress backgrounds to <200 KB
ACT-049 Fix 12 V01 (Too many visuals per page) findings in Trade
Location: Trade · Finding: V01		 Medium PBI Report Visual Analysis 28 visual containers (threshold: 15) Split into sub-pages or use bookmarks to show/hide groups of visuals
ACT-050 Fix 1 V03 (Excessive report-level filters) findings in Trade
Location: Trade · Finding: V03		 Medium PBI Report Visual Analysis 35 report-level filters (threshold: 10) Consolidate report-level filters. Move page-specific filters to page level
ACT-051 Fix 1 V05 (Auto-refresh or query reduction settings) findings in Trade
Location: Trade · Finding: V05		 Medium PBI Report Visual Analysis Query reduction settings not fully optimised: Cross-highlighting enabled — hovering on visuals generates additional DirectQuery queries; Slicer 'Apply' button disabled — each slicer selection immediately fires queries instead of batching; Filter 'Apply' button disabled — each filter change immediately fires queries In PBI Desktop → File → Options → Report settings → Query reduction: consider disabling cross-highlighting and enabling 'Apply' buttons on slicers and filters to reduce DirectQuery query volume during user interaction.
ACT-052 Fix 19 V06 (Wide tables/matrices) findings in Trade
Location: Trade · Finding: V06		 Medium PBI Report Visual Analysis matrix visual '0eac3eb0a1d581031ba4' has 43 projected columns (threshold: 10) Reduce column count to essential fields. Use drillthrough pages for detail views
ACT-053 Fix 12 V08 (Large embedded images) findings in Trade
Location: Trade · Finding: V08		 Low PBI Report Visual Analysis 1 image visual(s) Use URL-referenced images hosted on a CDN instead of embedded images. Compress backgrounds to <200 KB
ACT-054 Fix 74 FILTER_ALL anti-patterns
Location: 74 measures · Finding: FILTER_ALL		 High Semantic Model DAX Audit FILTER(ALL()) materialises the entire column into memory before filtering row by row. In DirectQuery this generates a full table scan subquery. Replace FILTER(ALL(Table[Column]), ...) with REMOVEFILTERS(Table[Column]) and a direct predicate in CALCULATE
ACT-055 Fix 54 NESTED_CALCULATE anti-patterns
Location: 54 measures · Finding: NESTED_CALCULATE		 High Semantic Model DAX Audit Contains 3 CALCULATE calls creating multiple context transitions. In DirectQuery, each nesting level generates an additional SQL subquery. Extract inner calculations into VAR variables or separate measures to reduce nesting
ACT-056 Fix 108 REPEATED_SUBEXPRESSION anti-patterns
Location: 108 measures · Finding: REPEATED_SUBEXPRESSION		 Medium Semantic Model DAX Audit Without VAR, the same subexpression is recalculated every time it appears, potentially doubling or tripling query cost. Use VAR to capture the repeated expression and reference the variable instead
ACT-057 Fix 12 MISSING_FORMAT_STRING anti-patterns
Location: 12 measures · Finding: MISSING_FORMAT_STRING		 Medium Semantic Model DAX Audit Without an explicit format string, PBI uses raw floating-point display, causing inconsistencies across visuals. Add a formatString (e.g. '#,0.00' for numbers, '0.0%' for percentages, '#,0' for integers)
ACT-058 Fix 14 DIVIDE_CALC anti-patterns
Location: 14 measures · Finding: DIVIDE_CALC		 Medium Semantic Model DAX Audit DIVIDE(CALCULATE(...), ...) forces the engine to evaluate the CALCULATE separately from the denominator, preventing single-pass aggregation. Each part generates its own DQ query. Use VAR to pre-compute CALCULATE result, then DIVIDE the variable
ACT-059 Fix 571 UNQUALIFIED_COLUMNS anti-patterns
Location: 571 measures · Finding: UNQUALIFIED_COLUMNS		 Low Semantic Model DAX Audit Unqualified column names can resolve ambiguously when tables share column names. Use 'TableName'[ColumnName] instead of bare [ColumnName]
ACT-060 Fix 212 NO_VARIABLES anti-patterns
Location: 212 measures · Finding: NO_VARIABLES		 Low Semantic Model DAX Audit Without VAR, intermediate calculations may be re-evaluated multiple times. VARs guarantee single evaluation. Use VAR/RETURN pattern to name intermediate calculations for readability and potential performance gains
ACT-061 Fix 38 HARDCODED_VALUES anti-patterns
Location: 38 measures · Finding: HARDCODED_VALUES		 Low Semantic Model DAX Audit Hardcoded literals make measures fragile and hard to maintain. Changes require editing each measure individually. Extract hardcoded values to a parameter table or separate measure for maintainability
